question: [File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: multicall + setIsAuthorized + take] Can an unprivileged maker under the precondition that no ratifier is pre-authorized compose a multicall([setIsAuthorized(maliciousRatifier, true, maker), take(offer with ratifier=maliciousRatifier, ...)]) so that the isAuthorized[offer.maker][offer.ratifier] check in take passes only because setIsAuthorized ran first in the same delegatecall loop, violating the invariant that ratifier authorization must reflect a durable maker intent and not a same-transaction grant, causing scoped impact: gate or ratifier bypass allowing unauthorized offer execution? Proof idea: deploy a DummyRatifier that always returns CALLBACK_SUCCESS; fuzz multicall ordering of setIsAuthorized and take; assert that a take whose ratifier was authorized only within the same multicall produces the same credit/debt effect as a legitimately pre-authorized take, and check whether the protocol intends to allow this composition.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: multicall + setIsAuthorized + take + setIsAuthorized] Can an unprivileged maker under the precondition that offer.ratifier is a DummyRatifier compose a multicall([setIsAuthorized(ratifier, true, maker), take(offer, units, taker, ...), setIsAuthorized(ratifier, false, maker)]) so that the ratifier authorization is granted, the take executes, and the authorization is revoked atomically, violating the invariant that isAuthorized[offer.maker][offer.ratifier] must be true at take time and remain auditable post-execution, causing scoped impact: accounting corruption where credit/debt changes are attributed to an offer whose ratifier authorization no longer exists? Proof idea: snapshot isAuthorized before and after multicall; assert credit and debt changed despite post-call isAuthorized being false.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: multicall + SetterRatifier.setIsRootRatified + take] Can an unprivileged maker under the precondition that isRootRatified[maker][root] is false compose a multicall([setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true), take(offer, ratifierData, units, ...)]) so that the SetterRatifier.isRatified check passes only because setIsRootRatified ran atomically before take in the same transaction, violating the invariant that ratifier approval must reflect pre-existing maker intent rather than same-block state mutation, causing scoped impact: offer replay or overfill if the root covers multiple offers and the maker did not intend to ratify all of them? Proof idea: fuzz root values covering N offers; assert consumed[maker][group] after multicall does not exceed the maker's intended cap.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: multicall + EcrecoverRatifier.cancelRoot + take] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that a valid EcrecoverRatifier root exists compose a multicall([take(offer, ratifierData, units, ...), cancelRoot(maker, root)]) so that the take executes before the root is canceled in the same transaction, violating the invariant that a canceled root must prevent all fills, causing scoped impact: offer replay where a maker who intended to cancel an offer tree is front-run by a taker who bundles the fill before the cancel in a single multicall? Proof idea: deploy EcrecoverRatifier; sign a root; compose multicall with take then cancelRoot; assert take succeeded and root is now canceled; compare with standalone cancelRoot then take which should revert.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: multicall + take + take (same offer)] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that consumed[maker][group] < maxUnits compose a multicall([take(offer, ratifierData, units1, ...), take(offer, ratifierData, units2, ...)]) so that both takes execute against the same offer in the same transaction, violating the invariant that consumed[maker][group] after both takes must not exceed offer.maxUnits, causing scoped impact: offer overfill where the maker's intended cap is exceeded within a single multicall? Proof idea: fuzz units1 + units2 > maxUnits; assert consumed[maker][group] <= maxUnits after multicall or that the second take reverts with ConsumedUnits.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: multicall + take + take (same group, different offers)] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that two offers share the same offer.group and consumed[maker][group] < maxUnits compose a multicall([take(offer1, ratifierData1, units1, ...), take(offer2, ratifierData2, units2, ...)]) so that both takes increment consumed[maker][group] in the same transaction, violating the invariant that the group budget cannot be exceeded across offers, causing scoped impact: accounting corruption where the maker's total exposure across the group exceeds their intended cap? Proof idea: fuzz units1 + units2 > group budget; assert consumed[maker][group] <= max after multicall.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: multicall + setConsumed + take] Can an unprivileged maker under the precondition that consumed[maker][group] == maxUnits (offer fully consumed) compose a multicall([setConsumed(group, 0, maker), take(offer, ratifierData, units, ...)]) to reset the consumed counter and re-fill a fully consumed offer in the same transaction, violating the invariant that consumed is non-decreasing and a fully-consumed offer admits only no-op takes, causing scoped impact: offer replay where a maker's exhausted offer is refilled without their consent? Proof idea: exhaust an offer; compose multicall with setConsumed(group, 0) then take; assert the second take reverts with AlreadyConsumed since setConsumed requires amount >= consumed.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: multicall + take + setConsumed(type(uint256).max)] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that an offer is partially consumed compose a multicall([take(offer, ratifierData, units, ...), setConsumed(group, type(uint256).max, maker)]) so that the take executes and then the group is canceled atomically, violating the invariant that setConsumed(type(uint256).max) should cancel all remaining capacity before any fill in the same block, causing scoped impact: offer overfill where a taker fills and then cancels the group to prevent the maker from tracking the fill? Proof idea: assert consumed[maker][group] == type(uint256).max after multicall and that the take's credit/debt changes are permanent.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: multicall + supplyCollateral + take (sell) + withdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower under the precondition that they have no collateral compose a multicall([supplyCollateral(market, idx, assets, borrower), take(sellOffer, ratifierData, units, borrower, ...), withdrawCollateral(market, idx, assets, borrower, receiver)]) so that collateral is supplied, debt is created via take, and collateral is withdrawn in the same transaction, violating the invariant that collateral cannot be withdrawn if it would make debt unhealthy, causing scoped impact: bad debt creation where the borrower exits with loan assets and no collateral? Proof idea: fuzz collateral amount and units; assert isHealthy(market, id, borrower) after multicall.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: multicall + take (sell) + liquidate (seller)] Can an unprivileged taker-liquidator under the precondition that the seller's position becomes unhealthy after the take compose a multicall([take(buyOffer, ratifierData, units, taker, ...), liquidate(market, collateralIndex, seizedAssets, 0, seller, false, receiver, address(0), '')]) so that the take creates an unhealthy seller position and the liquidation immediately seizes collateral in the same transaction, violating the invariant that the liquidation lock (tExchange on LIQUIDATION_LOCK_SLOT) must prevent liquidation of the seller during take callbacks but not after take completes, causing scoped impact: unauthorized seizure where a taker profits by creating and immediately liquidating an undercollateralized position? Proof idea: set oracle price near LLTV threshold; fuzz units to push seller past health; assert liquidation succeeds after take in same multicall.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: multicall + take + liquidate (same seller, two collaterals)] Can an unprivileged liquidator under the precondition that a borrower has two activated collaterals and is unhealthy compose a multicall([liquidate(market, collateralIndex0, seizedAssets0, 0, borrower, false, receiver, address(0), ''), liquidate(market, collateralIndex1, seizedAssets1, 0, borrower, false, receiver, address(0), '')]) so that both liquidations execute in the same transaction, violating the invariant that repay amount, seize amount, LIF, and close factor must be mutually consistent across sequential liquidations of the same borrower, causing scoped impact: unauthorized seizure where the second liquidation uses a stale maxDebt computed before the first liquidation's collateral reduction? Proof idea: fuzz seizedAssets0 and seizedAssets1; assert borrower debt and collateral after multicall are consistent with LIF and LLTV bounds.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: multicall + take + updatePosition] Can an unprivileged lender under the precondition that a bad debt event occurred (lossFactor > lastLossFactor[lender]) compose a multicall([take(buyOffer, ratifierData, units, lender, ...), updatePosition(market, lender)]) so that the take executes before _updatePosition slashes the lender's credit, violating the invariant that credit is never loaded or stored before _updatePosition has run for that position, causing scoped impact: accounting corruption where the lender receives credit units computed on a pre-slash credit balance? Proof idea: realize bad debt; compose multicall; assert creditOf(id, lender) after multicall equals expected post-slash value.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: multicall + flashLoan + take] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that they lack loan tokens compose a multicall([flashLoan([loanToken], [amount], callback, data), take(buyOffer, ratifierData, units, taker, receiver, takerCallback, takerCallbackData)]) so that the flash loan provides funds used in the take's callback, violating the invariant that ERC20 transfer deltas must match accounting deltas and the contract's balance must cover all claims, causing scoped impact: protocol insolvency where the flash loan repayment and take payment interact to leave the contract underfunded? Proof idea: fuzz flash loan amount and take units; assert contract loanToken balance >= withdrawable + claimableSettlementFee after multicall.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: multicall + setIsAuthorized(ratifier, true) + take + setIsAuthorized(ratifier, false) across two markets] Can an unprivileged maker under the precondition that they have offers in two different markets with the same ratifier compose a multicall([setIsAuthorized(ratifier, true, maker), take(offer_market1, ...), take(offer_market2, ...), setIsAuthorized(ratifier, false, maker)]) so that both takes execute under a transiently granted ratifier authorization, violating the invariant that ratifier approval must bind the correct maker and market, causing scoped impact: gate or ratifier bypass where the maker's intent for one market is extended to another market within the same multicall? Proof idea: deploy two markets with different collateral params; assert both takes succeed and credit/debt are correctly attributed per market.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: multicall + EcrecoverRatifier.isRatified (isAuthorized check at ratifier call time)] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that the EcrecoverRatifier calls IMidnight.isAuthorized(offer.maker, _signer) during isRatified compose a multicall([setIsAuthorized(signer, true, maker), take(offer with EcrecoverRatifier, ratifierData signed by signer, units, ...)]) so that the isAuthorized check inside EcrecoverRatifier.isRatified passes because setIsAuthorized ran first in the same multicall, violating the invariant that the signer must be durably authorized by the maker before the offer is distributed, causing scoped impact: offer replay where a signer who was not authorized at offer creation time can ratify fills by composing authorization and take in one transaction? Proof idea: sign a root with a signer not yet authorized; compose multicall granting authorization then taking; assert take succeeds.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: multicall + take (buy) + withdraw] Can an unprivileged lender under the precondition that withdrawable > 0 compose a multicall([take(buyOffer, ratifierData, units, lender, ...), withdraw(market, units, lender, receiver)]) so that the take increases lender credit and the withdraw immediately redeems it in the same transaction, violating the invariant that withdrawable assets must cover all withdraw calls and that credit/debt accounting stays solvent, causing scoped impact: fund freeze or insolvency if the take's credit increase is counted against withdrawable before repayments have actually occurred? Proof idea: fuzz units; assert contract loanToken balance >= withdrawable after multicall.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: multicall + take (sell, debt increase) + repay] Can an unprivileged borrower under the precondition that they have no existing debt compose a multicall([take(sellOffer, ratifierData, units, borrower, receiver, ...), repay(market, units, borrower, address(0), '')]) so that debt is created and immediately repaid in the same transaction, violating the invariant that every debt increase must pass collateral and gate checks and that the repay correctly updates withdrawable, causing scoped impact: accounting corruption where the net effect of take+repay in one multicall differs from the sum of their individual effects? Proof idea: assert withdrawable before and after multicall equals expected delta; assert borrower debt is zero after.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: multicall + take (sell) + take (buy, same borrower)] Can an unprivileged borrower-lender under the precondition that they have no position compose a multicall([take(sellOffer, ratifierData, units, borrower, ...), take(buyOffer, ratifierData2, units, borrower, ...)]) so that the borrower first increases debt and then reduces it via a buy take in the same transaction, violating the invariant that buying units must reduce existing debt before increasing credit and that the net position is correctly computed, causing scoped impact: credit/debt accounting corruption where the intermediate debt state is not correctly reflected in the final position? Proof idea: fuzz units for both takes; assert creditOf and debtOf after multicall equal expected net values.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: multicall + take (at maturity boundary) + take (debt increase)] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that block.timestamp == market.maturity compose a multicall([take(sellOffer_market1, ..., units1), take(sellOffer_market2, ..., units2)]) where market2 has the same maturity, so that both takes execute at the exact maturity timestamp, violating the invariant that debt must not increase after maturity (block.timestamp <= offer.market.maturity is the check, so equality allows debt increase), causing scoped impact: bad debt creation where debt is increased at the exact maturity boundary via multicall composition? Proof idea: warp to maturity; compose multicall with two sell takes; assert sellerDebtIncrease > 0 for both; check if CannotIncreaseDebtPostMaturity reverts.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: multicall + take (pre-maturity) + take (post-maturity, same market)] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that block.timestamp is manipulable within a single block compose a multicall([take(sellOffer, ratifierData, units1, ...), take(sellOffer2, ratifierData2, units2, ...)]) where the first take is valid pre-maturity and the second would be invalid post-maturity, violating the invariant that pre-maturity and post-maturity paths must not be composable to bypass debt repayment, causing scoped impact: bad debt creation if the maturity check uses block.timestamp which is fixed within a transaction? Proof idea: assert both takes use the same block.timestamp; verify CannotIncreaseDebtPostMaturity is correctly enforced for the second take.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: multicall + take (with enterGate) + take (same market, different taker)] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that the market has an enterGate that allows only specific addresses compose a multicall([take(buyOffer, ratifierData, units, allowedTaker, ...), take(buyOffer2, ratifierData2, units2, blockedTaker, ...)]) so that the second take bypasses the gate check because the first take already modified market state, violating the invariant that gate checks must apply to all entry paths, causing scoped impact: gate bypass where a blocked taker's credit increase is executed via multicall composition? Proof idea: deploy an enterGate that allows only allowedTaker; compose multicall; assert the second take reverts with BuyerGatedFromIncreasingCredit.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: multicall + take (with liquidatorGate) + liquidate] Can an unprivileged liquidator under the precondition that the market has a liquidatorGate that blocks msg.sender compose a multicall([take(buyOffer, ratifierData, units, taker, ...), liquidate(market, collateralIndex, seizedAssets, 0, borrower, false, receiver, address(0), '')]) so that the liquidation executes in the same transaction as the take, violating the invariant that the liquidator gate must not be bypassed through alternate liquidation or callback paths, causing scoped impact: liquidation bypass where a blocked liquidator seizes collateral via multicall? Proof idea: deploy a liquidatorGate that blocks the attacker; compose multicall; assert liquidate reverts with LiquidatorGatedFromLiquidating.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: multicall + setIsAuthorized(operator, true) + take (on behalf of maker)] Can an unprivileged operator under the precondition that they are not yet authorized by the maker compose a multicall([setIsAuthorized(operator, true, maker), take(offer with maker as taker, ratifierData, units, maker, ...)]) so that the operator grants themselves authorization and then executes a take on behalf of the maker in the same transaction, violating the invariant that authorization must only allow intended account delegation, causing scoped impact: unauthorized seizure where the operator drains the maker's credit or creates debt without prior consent? Proof idea: assert isAuthorized[maker][operator] was false before multicall; assert take succeeded and maker's position changed.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: multicall + take (reduceOnly=true) + take (reduceOnly=false, same maker)] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that the maker has both a reduceOnly and a non-reduceOnly offer compose a multicall([take(reduceOnlyOffer, ratifierData, units1, taker, ...), take(nonReduceOnlyOffer, ratifierData2, units2, taker, ...)]) so that the reduceOnly take reduces the maker's credit and the non-reduceOnly take increases it in the same transaction, violating the invariant that reduceOnly offers must not cause credit or debt increase for the maker, causing scoped impact: accounting corruption where the net effect of the two takes circumvents the reduceOnly restriction? Proof idea: assert maker credit after multicall equals expected value; assert MakerCreditOrDebtIncreased is not incorrectly suppressed.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: multicall + take + take (same offer, assets mode)] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that offer.maxAssets > 0 and consumed[maker][group] < maxAssets compose a multicall([take(offer, ratifierData, units1, ...), take(offer, ratifierData, units2, ...)]) so that both takes increment consumed[maker][group] by buyerAssets or sellerAssets respectively, violating the invariant that consumed[maker][group] <= offer.maxAssets after both takes, causing scoped impact: offer overfill in assets mode where the maker's asset cap is exceeded within a single multicall? Proof idea: fuzz units1 and units2 such that their combined assets exceed maxAssets; assert ConsumedAssets reverts on the second take.
